name: Chocolate-Dipped Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the sugar and melted butter. Add the Pernod, bourbon, and anise seed. Beat in the eggs, then stir in the nuts.
2. Sift the dry ingredients (flour and baking powder) together. Gently stir in the dry ingredients until well incorporated.
3. Cover with plastic wrap and chill for about three hours.
4. Preheat the oven to 375°F. Butter two cookie sheets.
5. Shape the dough on the cookie sheets into three loaves, well spaced. Each loaf should be about 2 inches wide and 1/2 inch thick.
6. Bake for about 20 minutes, until the loaves puff up and brown.
7. When the loaves are cool enough to touch, cut each loaf into diagonal slices about 1/2 inch thick. Lay the slices on their cut sides and toast them at 375°F for an additional 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Cool.
8. Melt the chocolate chips with shortening in a double boiler, stirring frequently. Remove from heat and stir until a candy thermometer reads 85°F.
9. Holding each cookie by its bottom, gently dip the tops into chocolate. Turn immediately and allow to dry, uncoated side down, on wax paper. Repeat until all are done.
